---
title: "Higher Ed Labor"
author: "Tom Ben-Shahar"
date: "February 2025"
output: pdf_document
---

Describe your issue very briefly here. Then show it with a minimal, self-contained example in the following R chunk.

```{r setup, cache=TRUE, warning=FALSE}

# Load Packages
pacman::p_load(tidyverse, broom, janitor, readr, stargazer, skimr, tidymodels, magrittr,doParallel, fixest, modelsummary, kableExtra, did, gt, beepr)

# Set directory
setwd("C:/Working_Projects/HigherEdLabor")

# Load Data
union <- read.csv("Written Data/union.csv")
pay <- read.csv("C:/Working_Projects/Research-Data-Storage/Data/IPEDS/combined_table.csv")

beep(1)
print("Setup Complete")

```

```{r data_cleaning, cache=TRUE, warning=FALSE}

# pay (combined_table) has one row per institution per year, union has multiple rows per institution per year (one for each union type)

# Join the two data frames, keeping all rows from `pay` and matching multiple rows from `union`
merged_data <- pay %>%
  left_join(union, by = "UNITID")


merged_data <- clean_names(merged_data, case = "lower_camel")


merged_data <- merged_data %>% 
  mutate(
    after_recog_date = ifelse(yr >= recognitionDate, 1, 0), # Indicator for if the union is recognized
    after_cba_date = ifelse(yr >= cbaDate, 1, 0), # Indicator for reached collective bargaining agreement
    after_recog_date = ifelse(recognitionDate %>% is.na(), 0, after_recog_date), 
    after_cba_date = ifelse(cbaDate %>% is.na(), 0, after_cba_date),
    endowment = coalesce(f1AEndowment, f2Endowment),# Combine the f1, f2, f3 predictors
    totalRevenue = coalesce(f1ATotalRevenue, f2TotalRevenue),
    totalGrants = coalesce(f1ATotalGrants, f2TotalGrants),
    expenses = coalesce(f1AExpenses, f2Expenses, f3Expenses),
    tuitionAndFees = coalesce(f1ATuitionAndFees, f2TuitionAndFees),
    totalAssets = coalesce(f1ATotalAssets, f2TotalAssets, f3TotalAssets),
    f1a_tuition_percap = f1ATuitionAndFees / ugTot, # Create a measure of tuition per student
    f1a_grants_percap = f1ATotalGrants / ugTot,
    f2_tuition_percap = f2TuitionAndFees / ugTot,
    f2_grants_percap = f2TotalGrants / ugTot,
    ft = ifelse(grepl("FT", unitComposition), 1, 0),# Add indicator for type of unit composition
    pt = ifelse(grepl("PT", unitComposition), 1, 0),
    ttt = ifelse(grepl("TTT", unitComposition), 1, 0),
    ntt = ifelse(grepl("NTT", unitComposition), 1, 0)

  # The rest will be done after the mdf transform to save compute time
    
  )

# Clean data frame so only 1 row per inst. year.
# Need different CBA dates for different union comps at same school

indicators <- c(
  "ft", "pt", "ttt", "ntt", "af_cba_n", "af_cba_t", "cba_date_t", "cba_date_n", "carnegie", "sector", "hasMedSchool", "hbcu",
  "tribal", "hasVet", "hasLaw", "hasEngineering", "hasDentistry", "hasBusiness"
)

faculty <- c(
  "instTotTR1", "instTotTR2", "instTotTR3", "instTotTR4", "instTotTR5", "instTotTR6", "instTotTR7",
  "instFtTR1", "instFtTR2", "instFtTR3", "instFtTR4", "instFtTR5", "instFtTR6", "instFtTR7",
  "instTotMR1", "instTotMR2", "instTotMR3", "instTotMR4", "instTotMR5", "instTotMR6", "instTotMR7",
  "instFtMR1", "instFtMR2", "instFtMR3", "instFtMR4", "instFtMR5", "instFtMR6", "instFtMR7",
  "instTotWR1", "instTotWR2", "instTotWR3", "instTotWR4", "instTotWR5", "instTotWR6", "instTotWR7",
  "instFtWR1", "instFtWR2", "instFtWR3", "instFtWR4", "instFtWR5", "instFtWR6", "instFtWR7",
  "avgSal9MoeqTR1", "avgSal9MoeqTR2", "avgSal9MoeqTR3", "avgSal9MoeqTR4", "avgSal9MoeqTR5", "avgSal9MoeqTR6", "avgSal9MoeqTR7",
  "avgSal9MoeqMR1", "avgSal9MoeqMR2", "avgSal9MoeqMR3", "avgSal9MoeqMR4", "avgSal9MoeqMR5", "avgSal9MoeqMR6", "avgSal9MoeqMR7",
  "avgSal9MoeqWR1", "avgSal9MoeqWR2", "avgSal9MoeqWR3", "avgSal9MoeqWR4", "avgSal9MoeqWR5", "avgSal9MoeqWR6", "avgSal9MoeqWR7"
)

keepers <- c("institutionName", "stateCode", "city", "x") # Removed "unitid", "yr", 

nonind <- setdiff(names(merged_data), c(indicators, "unitid", "yr")) # removed keepers and faculty

# This MIGHT BE WRONG.
# Gets rid of duplicate rows by summing things like different contract types 
# ^ This should be irrelevant since we expect combined_table to have one row per inst year
# and takes the max of any indicator in a yr
#
# Currently, merged_data is 1,000,000 lines, which is way more than we expect (in the 10,000s per year, a few hundred thousand total)
# This is using all my RAM to look at, and means that more data cleaning is needed.
# ^ Actually, MDF CLEAN is a reasonable length but with some missing data (2005-2006)
#
# Currently, the 2005-06 Access database has 7000 lines in HD, 37000 in SAL, and 445000 in the output table
# It appears that this problem only exists for the years where i removed CONTRACT from SQL, 2005 and 2006
#
# After picking just one contract type, the data looks reasonable in length
mdf_clean <- merged_data %>% 
  mutate(af_cba_t = ifelse(after_cba_date==1 & ttt==1, 1, 0),
         af_cba_n = ifelse(after_cba_date==1 & ntt==1, 1, 0),
         # add ttt and ntt specific date columns for did
         cba_date_t = ifelse(ttt==1, cbaDate, NA),
         cba_date_n = ifelse(ntt==1, cbaDate, NA)
         ) %>% 
  group_by(unitid, yr) %>% 
  summarise(across(all_of(nonind), first),
            #across(all_of(faculty), sum),
            #across(all_of(keepers), first), 
            across(all_of(indicators), max),
            .groups = "drop")
  
mdf_clean %<>% 
  mutate(
    logsal1 = log(avgSal9MoeqTR1),
    logsal2 = log(avgSal9MoeqTR2),
    logsal3 = log(avgSal9MoeqTR3),
    logsal4 = log(avgSal9MoeqTR4),
    logsal5 = log(avgSal9MoeqTR5),
    logsal6 = log(avgSal9MoeqTR6),
    logsal7 = log(avgSal9MoeqTR7)
  ) %>% 
  group_by(unitid) %>% 
  mutate(cba_ever = ifelse(any(after_cba_date == 1), 1, 0)) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  mutate(t = yr - cbaDate, 

    # Divide all avg sal measures by 9 for 2011 and earlier to correct for mistake in SQL code
    # Instead of multiplying by 9 to get avg sal for post 2011, divide total salary outlays by # of full time workers
    # Lets actually just abandon the entire 9moeq and do everything by the above method
    # 2015 and earlier, instTot is not reported, instead use isntFt
    avgSalTR1 = ifelse(yr <= 2015, totSalTR1 / instFtTR1,  totSalTR1 / instTotTR1), 
    avgSalTR2 = ifelse(yr <= 2015, totSalTR2 / instFtTR2,  totSalTR2 / instTotTR2), 
    avgSalTR3 = ifelse(yr <= 2015, totSalTR3 / instFtTR3,  totSalTR3 / instTotTR3), 
    avgSalTR4 = ifelse(yr <= 2015, totSalTR4 / instFtTR4,  totSalTR4 / instTotTR4), 
    avgSalTR5 = ifelse(yr <= 2015, totSalTR5 / instFtTR5,  totSalTR5 / instTotTR5), 
    avgSalTR6 = ifelse(yr <= 2015, totSalTR6 / instFtTR6,  totSalTR6 / instTotTR6), 
    avgSalTR7 = ifelse(yr <= 2015, totSalTR7 / instFtTR7,  totSalTR7 / instTotTR7), 

    avgSalMR1 = ifelse(yr <= 2015, totSalMR1 / instFtMR1,  totSalMR1 / instTotMR1), 
    avgSalMR2 = ifelse(yr <= 2015, totSalMR2 / instFtMR2,  totSalMR2 / instTotMR2), 
    avgSalMR3 = ifelse(yr <= 2015, totSalMR3 / instFtMR3,  totSalMR3 / instTotMR3), 
    avgSalMR4 = ifelse(yr <= 2015, totSalMR4 / instFtMR4,  totSalMR4 / instTotMR4), 
    avgSalMR5 = ifelse(yr <= 2015, totSalMR5 / instFtMR5,  totSalMR5 / instTotMR5), 
    avgSalMR6 = ifelse(yr <= 2015, totSalMR6 / instFtMR6,  totSalMR6 / instTotMR6), 
    avgSalMR7 = ifelse(yr <= 2015, totSalMR7 / instFtMR7,  totSalMR7 / instTotMR7), 

    avgSalWR1 = ifelse(yr <= 2015, totSalWR1 / instFtWR1,  totSalWR1 / instTotWR1), 
    avgSalWR2 = ifelse(yr <= 2015, totSalWR2 / instFtWR2,  totSalWR2 / instTotWR2), 
    avgSalWR3 = ifelse(yr <= 2015, totSalWR3 / instFtWR3,  totSalWR3 / instTotWR3), 
    avgSalWR4 = ifelse(yr <= 2015, totSalWR4 / instFtWR4,  totSalWR4 / instTotWR4), 
    avgSalWR5 = ifelse(yr <= 2015, totSalWR5 / instFtWR5,  totSalWR5 / instTotWR5), 
    avgSalWR6 = ifelse(yr <= 2015, totSalWR6 / instFtWR6,  totSalWR6 / instTotWR6), 
    avgSalWR7 = ifelse(yr <= 2015, totSalWR7 / instFtWR7,  totSalWR7 / instTotWR7)
  )

  mdf_clean %<>% 
    mutate(
      logsal1 = log(avgSalTR1),
      logsal2 = log(avgSalTR2),
      logsal3 = log(avgSalTR3),
      logsal4 = log(avgSalTR4),
      logsal5 = log(avgSalTR5),
      logsal6 = log(avgSalTR6),
      logsal7 = log(avgSalTR7)
    )

  beep(1) # Tell me when its done
  print("Data Cleaning Complete")

```



```{r data_vis, cache=TRUE, warning=FALSE}



# plot avg salaries to see if they look reasonable

## Data Prep
#--------------------------------------------

mdf_plot <- mdf_clean %>% 
  mutate(
    instTotTR1 = ifelse(yr <= 2015, instFtTR1, instTotTR1)
  ) %>%
  group_by(yr) %>% 
  summarise(
    avgSal1 = mean(avgSalTR1, na.rm = TRUE),
    avgSal2 = mean(avgSalTR2, na.rm = TRUE),
    avgSal3 = mean(avgSalTR3, na.rm = TRUE),
    avgSal4 = mean(avgSalTR4, na.rm = TRUE),
    avgSal5 = mean(avgSalTR5, na.rm = TRUE),
    avgSal6 = mean(avgSalTR6, na.rm = TRUE),
    avgSal7 = mean(avgSalTR7, na.rm = TRUE), 

    totSal1 = mean(totSalTR1, na.rm =TRUE), 
    instTot = mean(instTotTR1, na.rm=TRUE),
    instFt = mean(instFtTR1, na.rm = TRUE), 
    ft = mean(instFtTR1, na.rm =TRUE), 
    tot = mean(instTotTR1, na.rm=TRUE)
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("avgSal"), names_to = "Rank", values_to = "AvgSalary")


## Plot
#--------------------------------------------

ggplot(mdf_plot, aes(x = yr, y = AvgSalary, color = Rank)) + # I dont understand why this is fucked, it doesnt look like the data??
  geom_point() +
  geom_line() +
  labs(color = "Rank",
       title = "Average Salary by Rank",
       y = "Average Salary",
       x = "Year") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 2011) 

# There is a shift after 2011, which is when the data reporting shifts
# Remember that pre 2012, salaries are annual, and after salaries are monthly * 9. What if not everyone is on a 9 month contract?

ggplot(mdf_plot, aes(x = yr, y = totSal1)) + # This one looks normal # NOT since removing CONTRACT from SQL
geom_point()+
geom_line() +
labs(title = "Average Total Salary Expenditure for All Faculty", 
     y = "Total Salary Outlays (From totSalTR1)",
     x = "Year"
)

ggplot(mdf_plot, aes(x = yr, y = instTot)) + # Why is the avg num of staff so low between 2005-2011? In testdf, instTot is NA through 2015
geom_point()+
geom_line()+
labs(title = "Average Total Number of Faculty", 
     y = "Total Number of Faculty (From instTotTR1)",
     x = "Year"
)

ggplot(mdf_plot, aes(x = yr, y = ft)) + # Why is this the same as the above one??
geom_point()+
geom_line()+
labs(title = "Average Total Number of Faculty", 
     y = "Total Number of Faculty (From instFtTR1)",
     x = "Year"
)
# its because instTot isnt pulling from the right access table for those years (2005-2011)

# 2015 and earlier, instTot is not reported

ggplot(mdf_plot %>% filter(Rank == "avgSal1"), aes(x = yr, y = AvgSalary)) +
geom_point()+
geom_line() +
labs(title = "Average Salary by Dividing Total Salary by Total Faculty", 
     y = "Average Salary",
     x = "Year"
) # Jump after 2011 because data is different! This looks good but its NOT


# What if we make an indicator for "post2011" instead of trying to fix the data?


```

```{r test_regression, cache=TRUE, warning=FALSE}
# Lets see if the data is statistically different after 2011

mdf_reg <- mdf_clean %>%
  mutate(post2011 = ifelse(yr > 2011, 1, 0))

models <- lapply(c("avgSalTR1", "avgSalTR2", "avgSalTR3", "avgSalTR4", "avgSalTR5", "avgSalTR6", "avgSalTR7"), function(outcome) {
  feols(as.formula(paste("log(", outcome, ") ~ post2011")), data = mdf_reg, vcov = "hetero")
})

modelsummary(
  models,
  title = "Post-2011 Treatment Effect",
  output = "gt",
  shape = term ~ model,
  stars = TRUE
)

# Significance here tells us that there is likely something different about the data after 2011
# I can't find anything in post-2011 data that explains this

# Also, when i change the year for this indicator, the results do not change

```

```{r balance_table, cache=TRUE, warning=FALSE}
pre_survey_vars <- c(
  "f1AEndowment",
  "f2Endowment",
  "f2TotalAssets",
  "f1ATotalAssets",
  "applicantsTot",
  "instTotTR1",
  "instTotTR2",
  "instTotTR3",
  "instTotTR4",
  "instTotTR5",
  "instTotTR6",
  "instTotTR7",
  "avgSalTR1"
)


models <- lapply(pre_survey_vars, function(var) {
  feols(as.formula(paste("log(",var, ")~ cba_ever")), data = mdf_clean , vcov = "hetero")
})
names(models) <- pre_survey_vars

modelsummary(
  models,
  title = "Balance Table (in logs)",
  output = "gt",  # changed to gt for compatibility
  shape = model ~ term,
  stars = TRUE
)
```

```{r pre_trends, cache=TRUE, warning=FALSE}

outcomes <- c("avgSalTR1", "avgSalTR2", "avgSalTR3", "avgSalTR4", "avgSalTR5", "avgSalTR6", "avgSalTR7" )

models_pt <- lapply(outcomes, function(out) {
  feols(
    as.formula(paste0("log(",out, ")~ i(t, ref = 0) | unitid + yr")), data = mdf_clean
  )
})

modelsummary(
  models_pt,
  title = "Pre-Trends Test",
  output = "gt",
  shape = term~ model,
  stars = TRUE,
  coef_map = c(
    "t::-1" = "1 Year Before Treatment",
    "t::-2" = "2 Years Before Treatment",
    "t::-3" = "3 Years Before Treatment",
    "t::-4" = "4 Years Before Treatment",
    "t::-5" = "5 Years Before Treatment"
  )
)

# Plot
mdf_sum <- mdf_clean %>% 
  filter(!(after_cba_date)) %>% 
  group_by(cba_ever, yr) %>%
  summarise(
    tr1 = mean(avgSalTR1, na.rm = TRUE),
    tr2 = mean(avgSalTR2, na.rm = TRUE),
    tr3 = mean(avgSalTR3, na.rm = TRUE),
    tr4 = mean(avgSalTR4, na.rm = TRUE),
    tr5 = mean(avgSalTR5, na.rm = TRUE),
    tr6 = mean(avgSalTR6, na.rm = TRUE),
    tr7 = mean(avgSalTR7, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) 


plotsout <- lapply(c("tr1", "tr2", "tr3", "tr4", "tr5", "tr6", "tr7"), function(out) {
  ggplot(mdf_sum,aes(x = yr))+
  geom_smooth(aes(y = log(!!sym(out)), color = factor(cba_ever)))
})

# Don't forget that this puts out several plots


plotsout[1]
plotsout[2]
plotsout[3]
plotsout[4]
plotsout[5]
plotsout[6]
plotsout[7]

```

```{r did, cache=TRUE, warning=FALSE}
dep_vars <- c(
  "logsal1", 
  "logsal2", 
  "logsal3", 
  "logsal4", 
  "logsal5", 
  "logsal6", 
  "logsal7"
  )

plotsout <- lapply(dep_vars, function(dep){
  ggdid(aggte(att_gt(yname = dep,
                     tname = "yr",
                     idname = "unitid",
                     gname = "cba_date_n",
                     xformla = ~1,
                     est_method = "reg",
                     control_group = "notyettreated",
                     data = mdf_clean), type = "dynamic", na.rm=TRUE), title = dep)

})

plotsout[1]
plotsout[2]
plotsout[3]
plotsout[4]
plotsout[5]
plotsout[6]
plotsout[7]
```

```{r regressions, cache=TRUE, warning=FALSE}
# Regressions for each type of union
tr1g <- feols(log(avgSalTR1) ~after_cba_date |  yr + unitid, data = mdf_clean ) #tr1g denotes total (m+f) group 1 (all) grouped by union type
tr2g <- feols(log(avgSalTR2) ~af_cba_t |  yr + unitid, data = mdf_clean )
tr3g <- feols(log(avgSalTR3) ~af_cba_t |  yr + unitid, data = mdf_clean )
tr4g <- feols(log(avgSalTR4) ~af_cba_t |  yr + unitid, data = mdf_clean )
tr5g <- feols(log(avgSalTR5) ~af_cba_n |  yr + unitid, data = mdf_clean )
tr6g <- feols(log(avgSalTR6) ~af_cba_n |  yr + unitid, data = mdf_clean )
tr7g <- feols(log(avgSalTR7) ~af_cba_n |  yr + unitid, data = mdf_clean )

modelsummary(list("All instructional staff" = tr1g,
                  "Professor" = tr2g,
                  "Associate prof" = tr3g,
                  "Assistant prof" = tr4g),
             output = "kableExtra",
             stars = TRUE,
             title = "Tenior Track after CBA date")

modelsummary(list("Instructor" = tr5g,
                  "Lecturer"= tr6g,
                  "No Rank" = tr7g),
             output = "kableExtra",
             stars = TRUE,
             title = "Non Tenior Track after CBA date")
```

```{r test, cache=TRUE, warning=FALSE}
testdf <- mdf_clean %>%
  filter(
    #institutionName == "University of Oregon"
    unitid == 100654
  ) %>% 
  select(
    avgSal9MoeqTR1, 
    yr, 
    unitid, 
    institutionName, 
    ugTot, 
    instTotTR1, 
    totSalTR1, 
    instFtTR1, 
    avgSalTR1
  )%>% 
  mutate ( 
    avgSal = totSalTR1 / instFtTR1
  )


testdf2 <- merged_data %>% 
  filter(
    #institutionName == "University of Oregon"
    unitid == 100654
  ) %>% 
  select(
    avgSal9MoeqTR1, 
    yr, 
    unitid, 
    institutionName, 
    ugTot, 
    instTotTR1
  ) 
testdf3 <- pay %>% 
  filter(
    #institutionName == "University of Oregon"
    UNITID == 100654
  ) %>% 
  select(
    YR, 
    UNITID, 
    inst_ft_m_r1, 
    inst_tot_t_r1
  )


```

## THESIS STUFF:

```{r thesis, cache=TRUE, warning=FALSE}

# Import csv of state legislation
# Add legislation as a factor indicator (4 types) to each row
# Regress endowment (may have to add from Access data) on legislation indicator with inst/yr fe
# Also do DiD analysis since it makes more sense here

thesis_treatment <- read.csv("../Thesis/transparency.csv")

thesis_treatment %<>%
  rename(stateCode = "scode")

mdf_th <- mdf_clean %>% 
  left_join(thesis_treatment, by = "stateCode")

mdf_th %<>% 
  mutate(
    explicit = ifelse(Transparency == "Explicit", 1, 0),
    Transparency = as.factor(Transparency) 
         )

mdf_th$Transparency <- relevel(mdf_th$Transparency, ref = "None")

m1 <- feols(log(f1AEndowment) ~explicit |  yr , data = mdf_th , cluster = "stateCode", vcov = "hetero")
m2 <- feols(log(f2Endowment) ~explicit |  yr, data = mdf_th , cluster = "stateCode", vcov = "hetero")

modelsummary(list("Public" = m1,
                  "Private" = m2),
             output = "kableExtra",
             stars = TRUE)

```
